title: EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE - 211info
text: BEAVERTON CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Employment Related Day Care program (ERDC) helps families who are working, in school, or receiving Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) pay for child care, including registration fees. 15425 NW Greenbrier Parkway Beaverton, OR 970066002 Eligibility: This program is for families that: -Live in Oregon -Are working or in school or receiving TANF. -In a two-parent family, both parents must be working or in school. (This does not apply to parents receiving TANF.) -There are some exceptions for two parent households where one parent cannot provide child care because of a medical or mental health condition, or if supervised contact is required by the State. -Need child care to keep working, attending school, or participating in family well-being activities. -Have a child who is: -A US citizen or qualifying non-citizen, -Under 13 years old and needs child care, or -13 to 17 years old and needs care because of special circumstances -Meet income limits. -Initial income limits 200% Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Ongoing and Exit limit 250% FPL. See website for income chart. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 646-9952 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 646-9952 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply online, by phone, or in person at a local ODHS Self-Sufficiency Branch Office FEES: ERDC is a subsidy program. This means many families still pay part of the child care cost. This is called a copayment (copay). The copay amount depends on family size and income. DOCUMENTS: Proof of age, household income, and address LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus line 67 Services: Child Care Expense Assistance Child Care Expense Assistance * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD ABUSE REPORTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE SERVICES MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BEAVERTON CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY
